description: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) version 4.0.1 provides a baseline of technical and operational requirements designed to protect payment account data.

requirements.name: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
requirements.description: Network security controls (NSCs) such as firewalls and other network security technologies are points of policy enforcement that control network traffic between two or more logical or physical network segments.

requirements.name: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
requirements.description: Malicious individuals use default passwords and other vendor default settings to compromise systems. These passwords and settings are well known and can be easily determined.

requirements.name: Protect Stored Account Data
requirements.description: Protection methods such as encryption, truncation, masking, and hashing are critical components of stored account data protection.

requirements.name: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
requirements.description: Sensitive information must be encrypted during transmission over networks that are easily accessed by malicious individuals.

requirements.name: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
requirements.description: Malicious software (malware) is software installed without the entity knowledge or consent for the purpose of compromising the security of the entity systems and data.

requirements.name: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
requirements.description: Security vulnerabilities in systems and software may allow criminals to access cardholder data. Many of these vulnerabilities are eliminated by installing vendor-provided security patches.

requirements.name: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
requirements.description: To ensure critical data can only be accessed by authorized personnel, systems and processes must be in place to limit access based on need to know and according to job responsibilities.

requirements.name: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
requirements.description: Two fundamental principles of identifying and authenticating users: establishing the identity of a user or process, and proving or verifying the user identity is genuine.

requirements.name: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
requirements.description: Any physical access to cardholder data or systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data provides the opportunity for individuals to access and/or remove devices, data, systems, or hardcopies.

requirements.name: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
requirements.description: Logging mechanisms and the ability to track user activities are critical in preventing, detecting, and minimizing the impact of a data compromise.

requirements.name: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
requirements.description: Vulnerabilities are being discovered continually by malicious individuals and researchers. System components, processes, and bespoke and custom software should be tested frequently.

requirements.name: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
requirements.description: A strong security policy sets the security tone for the whole entity and informs personnel what is expected of them.
